article_title: Activist Investors and the Governance Pendulum
news_headline: 2026 Proxy Season Trends: The Fracturing of Shareholder Power
post_text:
The 2026 proxy season was supposed to signal a retreat of shareholder power. It did the opposite — just in unexpected ways.

Proxy contests are down. But Elliott Management pressured Norwegian Cruise Line into adding five new board members without a single shareholder vote. JANA Partners pushed Six Flags into a new board chair. Activists are winning the governance war while bypassing the traditional battlefield entirely.

This is exactly the dynamic our writers explored in 'Activist Investors and the Governance Pendulum' — the idea that the tension between shareholder rights and management authority doesn't resolve, it just shifts form. When formal mechanisms weaken, informal pressure fills the vacuum. The pendulum keeps swinging.

If you want the analytical framework behind what's making headlines this proxy season, the WBR piece holds up remarkably well.

Link in comments. #CorporateGovernance #ShareholderActivism
